program_overview.title: Overview of the HCI Graduate Program
program_overview.description: The study of the relationship between humans and computers has quickly become one of the most dynamic and significant fields of technical investigation. Iowa State University’s graduate program in Human Computer Interaction (HCI) is an established leader in this rapidly evolving field, making strategic investments to accelerate research, attract talented students and faculty, and expand the program of study.
key_characteristics: Impacts nearly every area of lives
Reflects broad recognition in academia and industry of the need to train researchers for technological progress
Benefits from interactions with ISU faculty from every college and researchers at the VRAC (research center)

learning_goals.title: HCI Graduate Program Learning Goals
goals: Design and evaluate systems with a user-centered design lens
Participate in a multidisciplinary product development team
Choose and apply an appropriate user-centered design approach
Identify key players and organizations in HCI
Use theories and models in the HCI field
